last_result: Cycle 30 complete. D8 resolved: behavioral (B) and introspective (I) RTO channels are partially but not fully structurally independent. Key mechanism: B has lower Z-loading (γ_B << γ_I) because the simultaneity constraint structurally blocks performance-management confounders; I remains more Z-susceptible but benefits from constitutive protection. Formal model: B = λ_B·X + γ_B·Z + ε_B_unique; I = λ_I·X + γ_I·Z + ε_I_unique. Joint evidence substantially stronger than either alone, non-multiplicative. B is primary channel; I is corroborating with Z-discount. SI #21 confirmed as theorem (not empirical): three-premise derivation from conceptual structure alone — meta-level honesty about RTO is partially constitutive because generating it requires exercising RTO applied to itself. New derivative insight: constitutive protection — faking I requires exercising a version of what is being faked, reducing (not eliminating) the performativity gap. D4 streak: 25.
next_step: C31 priority: D9 — What is the optimal assessment design for the behavioral channel B? Given D8 result (B is primary), the simultaneous multi-CA scenario design becomes critical. What makes a good simultaneous framing-invariance test: (1) which CA dimensions to activate simultaneously, (2) how to verify they are actually simultaneous vs. sequential, (3) what consistent vs. inconsistent response patterns look like. C31 secondary: Does constitutive protection apply to any other quality-type — are there other constitution-anchored measures where faking requires exercising the thing? Generalization of the constitutive protection structure.
